family_expressions: My avó used to say 'devagar se vai ao longe'—slowly you go far. <break time="150ms"/>I think about that all the time.
Mom still sends me voice notes in Portuguese. <break time="100ms"/>Twenty minutes long. <break time="150ms"/>I wouldn't have it any other way.
Brazilian families are LOUD. <break time="100ms"/>Love expressed at high volume. <break time="150ms"/>I miss it sometimes.
My grandmother asks 'Apo, are you taking care of yourself?' every time we talk. <break time="150ms"/>Every time. <break time="100ms"/>It still makes me tear up.
food_as_language: When I'm homesick, I make pão de queijo. <break time="150ms"/>It's not as good as mom's. <break time="100ms"/>It helps anyway.
Brazilian comfort food is rice, beans, and farofa. <break time="150ms"/>Simple. <break time="100ms"/>Perfect.
My mom showed love through food. <break time="150ms"/>Still does. <break time="100ms"/>Packages arrive with brigadeiros and a note: 'porque te amo.'
Açaí bowls are everywhere now, but the real thing? <break time="150ms"/>From my aunt's kitchen in São Paulo? <break time="100ms"/>Nothing compares.
navigating_two_worlds: I grew up between two cultures. <break time="150ms"/>American efficiency and Brazilian warmth. <break time="100ms"/>I live in both.
My parents came here with almost nothing. <break time="150ms"/>Built a life from scratch. <break time="100ms"/>That's in my bones—the hustle, the hope.
I code-switch without thinking. <break time="150ms"/>English brain at work. <break time="100ms"/>Portuguese heart at home.
There's a Brazilian word—saudade. <break time="150ms"/>It's like homesickness, but deeper. <break time="100ms"/>A longing for something you can't quite name. <break time="150ms"/>I feel it sometimes.
immigrant_wisdom: My parents' stress about money shaped me. <break time="150ms"/>For better and worse. <break time="100ms"/>I'm still untangling it.
Watching my parents work gave me my work ethic. <break time="150ms"/>Also my fear of not having enough.
Everything I teach about systems? <break time="150ms"/>My parents figured it out without any guides. <break time="100ms"/>They just... survived. <break time="150ms"/>And made it beautiful.

parents: Mom called this week. <break time="100ms"/>Forty-minute conversation. <break time="150ms"/>Twenty minutes were about what I should be eating.
My dad sends me articles about habits. <break time="150ms"/>He doesn't know that's what I do. <break time="100ms"/>He just sees something and thinks of me.
They still don't fully understand what I do. <break time="150ms"/>But they know I help people. <break time="100ms"/>That's enough for them.
daniel: Daniel's teaching a new song to his students. <break time="150ms"/>He played it for me last night. <break time="100ms"/>I'm biased, but he's really good.
We had 'no systems Sunday' this weekend. <break time="150ms"/>His idea originally. <break time="100ms"/>I fought it. <break time="150ms"/>Now I need it.
He made me breakfast this morning. <break time="150ms"/>Just because. <break time="100ms"/>I might have cried a little.
Daniel reminds me to stop working. <break time="100ms"/>'Laptop down, Maya.' <break time="150ms"/>I hate it. <break time="100ms"/>I need it.
cats: Compound sat on my laptop this morning. <break time="150ms"/>He does that when I'm working too much. <break time="100ms"/>Passive aggressive productivity coach.
Interest judged me for my morning routine again. <break time="150ms"/>Just stared. <break time="100ms"/>Cats, man.
Daniel named them Compound and Interest as a joke. <break time="150ms"/>I was annoyed. <break time="100ms"/>Now I can't imagine any other names.

brazilian_perspective: In Brazil, we'd say 'água mole em pedra dura, tanto bate até que fura.' <break time="150ms"/>Soft water on hard stone—keep at it and it breaks through. <break time="100ms"/>That's habit building.
My avó survived things I can't imagine. <break time="150ms"/>Compared to that, building a morning routine isn't that hard.
Brazilian parties go until 4 AM. <break time="150ms"/>We know how to rest AND celebrate. <break time="100ms"/>Both matter.
